Songyan Hou

The only thing certain is the uncertainty

About me

Hi, I am Songyan Hou, a master student in applied mathematics in the department of mathematics at ETH, studying stochastic finance under the supervision of Prof. Christoph Czichowsky. Before coming to Zurich, I obtained my bechelor degree in mathematics in Nanjing University under the supervion of Prof. Zhang, Qiang on numerical methods of PDEs.

Interests

I'm interested in Stochastic Finance and numerical methods implemented on stochastic models. Currently I am mainly studying on the topics of stimulating rough mathematical finance problem with deep neural network.

Articles

Artificial Neural Networks

In this article, we give an introduction of the structures of artificial neural networks(ANNs), especially the rectified ANNs with analysis on operations between ANNs and show some concrete examples of ANNs.

Posted by Songyan Hou on February 15, 2019

Generators of random variables

In this article, we briefly introduce some generators of random variables and present the realization detail of some simple but commonly used distribution and attach the Matlab codes.

Posted by Songyan Hou on December 25, 2018

Dynkin's Lemma with Applications

In this article we introduce Dynkin's Lemma with their application. In Dynkin's Lemma used proof, we define a so called Dynkin's property have an insight of what difficulty does Dynkin's Lemma really helps to overcome and under what condition Dynkin's lemma really help. Besides the use of a Dynkin's property language shorten the proof without repeating programized arguments of using Dynkin's Lemma.

Posted by Songyan Hou on September 22, 2018

WENO and WPENO Methods with Applications

In this article we discuess two extension of essentially non-oscillatory methods, WENO method and WPENO method with their applications on conservation law. Both ofthem are designed to obtain high order of accuracy meanwhile maintaining the shock capturing features mainly for convection dominating equations.

Posted by Songyan Hou on September 22, 2018

FEATURED TAGS
WENO Probability Theory SDE Numerical Analysis ANNs
